methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $63M total raised proxy.

private.name: Havoc AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Ocean & Maritime), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Ulysses Ecosystem Engineering
private.rationale: Same sector (Ocean & Maritime), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Bedrock Ocean is a Series A-stage ocean and maritime technology company founded by Anthony DiMare and Charles Chiau that has raised $63M to date. The valuation landscape for this sector remains sparse, with only two identifiable private peers (Havoc AI and Ulysses Ecosystem Engineering) and no directly comparable public benchmarks available for triangulation.

Given the limited comp universe, the analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage capital rounds. Using total raised ($63M) as a proxy for post-money valuation, this method yields an implied valuation of $126M. This approach is mechanically sound but should be weighted carefully given the absence of sector-specific peer validation.

The triangulated range spans $107M (low) to $145M (median $126M), with the single-method constraint creating material uncertainty bands. The key limitation is methodological dependency: the analysis cannot validate whether a 2.0x multiplier reflects market expectations for ocean tech specifically, or whether the two private peers would support higher or lower stepping conventions. Investors should treat $126M as a working reference point pending either: (i) expansion of the comparable set within maritime technology, or (ii) acquisition of additional financial metrics (revenue, burn, runway) to ground relative valuation approaches. The sparse public market for this sector argues for conservative reliance on this single methodology.
